question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/HookStorageRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyInAndDeletedIsFalse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted list of 32-byte keys that, after left-padding normalization in HookStorage.setKey(), collide with staking-reward storage slots belonging to a different ownerId, causing the repository to return pending reward values the attacker should not see?
